message:
feat(indexer): land content-addressed memoization vocabulary (bead ibuuh.34)

First slice of bead ibuuh.34: introduce the types and invariants for
content-addressed memoization without yet wiring them into the
rebuild pipeline. The downstream slice migrates the
ConversationPacket-driven dataflow (bead ibuuh.32) and then hooks
`ContentAddressedMemoCache` into the lexical normalization, token
extraction, and semantic-embedding paths — all of which currently
recompute the same derivations repeatedly for identical content
(historical salvage replay, boilerplate tool banter across sessions,
semantic rebuilds after a version bump).

- New module `src/indexer/memoization.rs`. `#![allow(dead_code)]` at
  module scope because this slice lands the vocabulary only; the
  downstream slice will flip each call site and the warnings will
  fall off naturally as usage appears. The module-level comment and
  doc comment both spell out that this is intentional.
- Register the module in `src/indexer/mod.rs` as `pub(crate)` so
  future in-tree callers can use it while it remains unexported from
  the crate surface until we're confident in the API shape.

Invariants the types enforce (documented in the module doc):

1. Memo keys *always* combine content hash with
   `(algorithm, algorithm_version)`. A version bump of any derivation
   automatically invalidates prior cache entries — silent cross-
   version reuse is impossible by construction.
2. Quarantined entries stay resident (so we can audit why something
   was quarantined and when) but are never served to callers; the
   audit log records the transition.
3. Bounded LRU with structured hit/miss/evict/quarantine audit events
   so observability is a first-class feature rather than bolted on
   later.

No runtime callers yet; `cargo check` / `cargo test` exercise the
types through the module's own unit tests only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
